## [00:00 - 01:59]

We need to talk about this. So if you're an advertiser, I'm sure you've seen this article already or a similar article like it. And I know it sounds scary. And the thing is, look, yeah, there is reason for concern, but it's really tough to say for sure because it also feels like we're just not getting all the information. So this article says that the European data regulator will extend its ban on Facebook and Instagram, preventing those apps from using behavioral advertising within two weeks. And what's weird is that Meta just announced a paid ad-free subscription option within the EU and in that announcement, they specifically called this out as to why they're offering it in the first place. It was in response to these regulations and that option would then provide consent. I'll just say it would be weird if Meta announced these subscriptions for this purpose without getting approval first, without getting that clearance from regulators that this would do the thing. And of course they have a huge legal team that would look over this announcement. So what's gonna happen in two weeks? I mean, it's tough to say, but I'm going to bet it's extremely unrealistic to expect that Meta's gonna shut down behavioral targeting within that time without major disruption. But yeah, the European Union is absolutely an area that we need to be watching when it comes to behavioral targeting and privacy loss. That said, I would be surprised if there's some targeting apocalypse within the next two weeks. And I'm no legal expert, but it sure sounds like Meta was in the process of getting all this sorted out before this announcement just came about. We'll see, keep an eye on it, but it's obviously gonna have broader impact in the long term. So what do you think?
